ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for yuan yang coffee frappuccino specifically; closest parent 'coffee beverage' (FOODON_03301710) used as anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for blended iced yuan yang coffee frappuccino; AN000A (Coffee and coffee substitutes) is the closest defensible parent category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall-size (354 ml) Yuan Yang Coffee Frappuccino as served at Starbucks Singapore, weighing approximately 370 g including ice and blended ingredients, served without whipped cream.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Tall Yuan Yang Coffee Frappuccino Blended Beverage (no whip) — Nutrition Information
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central — Branded Foods (Starbucks nutritional data submitted to USDA); cross-referenced with Starbucks Singapore published nutrition panel
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Yuan Yang Coffee Frappuccino is a Singapore-specific Starbucks menu item combining coffee, hojicha, milk, and java chips without whipped cream. Nutrient values are estimated by adapting USDA FoodData Central data for Starbucks Coffee Frappuccino Blended Beverage (NDB 45224567) and adjusting for the hojicha component and absence of whipped cream. Per-100g values are derived from a 370 g tall serving. Energy and macros are consistent with comparable Starbucks blended coffee beverages in the USDA Branded Foods database. Values represent a best-estimate adaptation; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — milk-based blended coffee beverages; Starbucks Coffee Frappuccino analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Yuan Yang Coffee Frappuccino. GI of 53 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for milk-based blended coffee beverages and Starbucks Coffee Frappuccino analogues, which report GI values in the range 46–58 (low category). The dominant glycemic contributors are sugar syrup, java chips, and milk lactose. Hojicha contributes negligible glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (53 × 49.95) / 100 ≈ 26.5, rounded to 26.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~148 mg per tall serving) and caffeine
hypertension.guidance: A single tall serving contributes approximately 148 mg sodium (6% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), which is modest; however, individuals with hypertension should be aware of caffeine content (~75 mg per tall serving), which may cause transient blood pressure elevation, and should limit consumption to one serving per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~44 g free sugars per tall serving) and significant glycemic load (~26.6)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 44 g of free sugars and a glycemic load of ~26.6 per tall serving, this beverage can cause a substantial postprandial blood glucose rise and is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; if consumed, portion should be reduced to a short size and frequency limited to occasional treats.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from milk and java chips, and high free sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each tall serving provides approximately 3.7 g saturated fat and 44 g free sugars; regular consumption may contribute to dyslipidaemia and elevated triglycerides, and individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake to occasional consumption and consider requesting a reduced-sugar or smaller-size option.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yuan Yang Coffee Frappuccino is a Singapore-exclusive Starbucks blended iced beverage that fuses the classic Hong Kong yuan yang (鸳鸯) concept — a traditional blend of coffee and milk tea — with Starbucks' Frappuccino format. This version incorporates espresso-based coffee, hojicha (Japanese roasted green tea), creamy whole milk, java chips (chocolate-flavoured chips), and ice, all blended together and served without whipped cream. The result is a layered flavour profile combining the bitterness of coffee, the earthy roasted notes of hojicha, the sweetness of chocolate chips, and the creaminess of milk. The beverage is ultra-processed and high in free sugars (~44 g per tall serving), making it a high-concern item for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its significant glycemic load (~26.6). It is moderate concern for cardiovascular disease owing to saturated fat from milk and java chips. Sodium content is modest (~148 mg per serving). The drink is vegetarian and halal-certified at Starbucks Singapore. It is not suitable for vegans or those with dairy or gluten allergies. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 75 kcal, 1.9 g protein, 1.6 g fat, and 13.5 g carbohydrate. The yuan yang concept originates from Hong Kong cha chaan teng culture and has been adapted into this premium blended format for the Singapore market.

Is Yuan Yang Frappuccino suitable for diabetics?
Does Yuan Yang Frappuccino have whipped cream?

Yuan Yang Frappuccino nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: Yuan Yang Coffee Frappuccino is a Singapore-specific Starbucks menu item not directly listed in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as an exact entry. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA Branded Foods data for comparable Starbucks blended coffee beverages and adjusted for the hojicha component and no-whip preparation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for milk-based blended coffee beverages. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per-100g estimates derived from a 370 g tall serving (354 ml cup with ice and blended ingredients). 2. The beverage is served without whipped cream as stated in the input description. 3. Vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12), folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and selenium are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the USDA Branded Foods reference entries for comparable Starbucks blended coffee beverages and are not available from the adapted source. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace per USDA Branded Foods data for comparable Starbucks products. 5. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per serving = (53 × 49.95) / 100 ≈ 26.6. 6. The yuan yang concept originates from Hong Kong cha chaan teng culture; this Starbucks Singapore adaptation incorporates hojicha as a tea component rather than traditional Hong Kong milk tea. 7. Halal status reflects Starbucks Singapore's general halal certification; individual outlet certification should be verified. 8. Price SGD 9.50 preserved from input (field survey data, Starbucks One Fullerton).
